m_Item1.QuestionText: Servlet mapping defines ___________

Options.OptionText: An association between a URL pattern and a servlet name
An association between a URL pattern and a request page
An association between a URL pattern and a response page

m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet mapping is the mapping between servlet name and URL pattern which can be written in the form of @WebServlet annotation or in web.xml file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the full name of J2EE?
m_Item1.Explanation: J2EE - Java 2 Enterprise Edition

m_Item1.QuestionText: Select the correct statement

Options.OptionText: ServletConfig is not available inside constructor
servlet gets instantiated every time a new request comes.
programmer has to override “service()” method of parent class

m_Item1.Explanation: ServletConfig is not available inside constructor. ServletConfig object is available in init method

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is not a parameter type
m_Item1.Explanation: session is not a parameter.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Select the wrong statement

Options.OptionText: ServletConfig is not available inside constructor
servlet gets instantiated every time a new request comes.
programmer need not override “service()” method of parent class, instead he can override required doXXX() methods.

m_Item1.Explanation: Servlets get instantiated only once at a time first request comes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is not an attribute
m_Item1.Explanation: init is a method in servlet which is not a attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following attributes are thread-safe?
m_Item1.Explanation: request-scope variables are thread-safe because they are created per new  request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following method is not used to retrieve request parameters?
m_Item1.Explanation: To retrive single request parameter then used getParameter() or for multiple use getParameterValues().

m_Item1.QuestionText: Given that the service() method of a typical servlet is multithreaded, which one of the following issues does NOT need to be addressed in a servlet's implementation?
m_Item1.Explanation: Variables declared in the methods are thread safe as each thread creates it own Program Stack and function variables are allocated in the stack. This means that variable in a methods are created for each thread, hence does not have any thread sync issues associated.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods is not the part of servlet lifecycle.
m_Item1.Explanation: start method is not a part of servlet lifecycle.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one of the following is not a servlet attribute scope?
m_Item1.Explanation: config is not the scope for setting attributes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the followings is must for a servlet class

Options.OptionText: it must have public no-arg constructor

m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet class must have public no-arg constructor.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main difference between servlet and cgi?

Options.OptionText: Servlets are thread based , cgi are process based
Servlets execute slower compare to cgi
Servlets have platform specific API, whereas cgi has no platform specific API

m_Item1.Explanation: Main difference between Servlet and cgi is as follows:  Servlets are thread based , cgi are process based

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following should be used to share data between all servlets in a web application?
m_Item1.Explanation: In a web application, context attributes are accessible for all the servlets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements is correct?

Options.OptionText: The response from the dedicated server to a HEAD request consists of status line, content type header and content body.
The response from the server to a GET request does not contain a document.

The setStatus method defined in the HttpServletRequest class takes int as an argument and sets
the status of Http response.

The HttpServletResponse defines constants like SC_NOT_FOUND that may be used as a
parameter to setStatus method.

m_Item1.Explanation:
The HttpServletResponse defines constants like SC_OK, SC_NOT_FOUND etc that may be used as a
parameter to setStatus method.
setStatus() method is in request. Get method can have document. Head request does not have content body.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If the request method is “get”, which method is called
m_Item1.Explanation: doGet() method is called when the request method is get.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Select the correct statement.

Options.OptionText: CGI creates different process for each request

m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet is multithreaded. CGI is process based so creates different process for each request. HttpServlet extends GenericServlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In order to make Servlet entry inside DD(Deployment Descriptor), we need to mention _______.
m_Item1.Explanation: To add servlet in web.xml, we need to use <servlet-class>,<servlet-name>,<url-pattern> tags.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Request and response are created for each thread
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet is multithreaded so request and response are created for each thread.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is one per servlet
m_Item1.Explanation: Config is an object which is created per servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Life cycle methods in case of servlet are
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet life cycle methods are init, service and destroy.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Service method is declared to throw ____
m_Item1.Explanation: service() method throws Servlet and IO Exception.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Deployment Descriptor in web application is _______.
m_Item1.Explanation: Java web applications use a deployment descriptor file to determine how URLs map to servlets, which URLs require authentication, and other information. This file is named web.xml, and resides in the app's WAR under the WEB-INF/ directory. web.xml is part of the servlet standard for web applications

m_Item1.QuestionText: getWriter() method is invoked on ______ .
m_Item1.Explanation: response.getWriter() method is used for getting writing stream on response object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HttpServlet class is abstract class
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTPServlet supports http protocol only and it is an abstract class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: init() calls init(ServletConfig)
m_Item1.Explanation: init() does not calls init(ServletConfig)

m_Item1.QuestionText: Init method has argument:
m_Item1.Explanation: init(ServletConfig config)

m_Item1.QuestionText: In order to read config parameters which method is used _______.
m_Item1.Explanation: To read config parameter use getInitParameter() method of ServletConfig object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If u want to ensure servlet loading even before first request u need to use
m_Item1.Explanation: The load-on-startup element of web-app loads the servlet at the time of deployment or server start if value is positive.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse can be used as thread-safe objects
m_Item1.Explanation: Yes. HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse are thread-safe

m_Item1.QuestionText: HttpSession gets migrated to another JVM if required
m_Item1.Explanation: Yes. HttpSession can be migrated to another JVM if required in a clustered environment

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one is one per context/application?
m_Item1.Explanation: ServletContext is an object created per application or context.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ServletConfig is available inside constructor
m_Item1.Explanation: ServletConfig is available in init method not inside the constructor

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method of the servlet is/are called several times in its life ?
m_Item1.Explanation: E

m_Item1.QuestionText: ....................... is sub element of <servlet> represents the name of the servlet.
m_Item1.Explanation:
<servlet> element structure in web.xml:
<servlet>
    <servlet-name></servlet-name>
    <servlet-class></servlet-class>
</servlet>

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is javax.servlet.Servlet ?
m_Item1.Explanation: Any user defined servlet should implement Servlet interface directly or indirectly.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about HTTP Get method ?

Options.OptionText: The GET method sends the encoded user information appended to the page request
The GET method is the default method to pass information from browser to web server

m_Item1.Explanation:
GET is used to request data from a specified resource.
Note that the query string (name/value pairs) is sent in the URL of a GET request:
Example : /test/check?name1=value1&name2=value2

m_Item1.QuestionText: A deployment descriptor describes ___________
m_Item1.Explanation:
Web application in Java use a deployment descriptor file to determine web component settings and this file is named web.xml, and resides in the app's WAR under the WEB-INF/ directory. web.xml is part of the servlet standard for web applications.
Deployment descriptor describes the classes, resources and the configuration for the application as well as the information on the usage of web server to serve the requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When doPost() method of servlet gets called?

Options.OptionText: When the form is submitted to server side
The service() method checks the HTTP request type as POST and calls doPost() method

m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet container is responsible for routing requests to the appropriate servlet based on URL mappings and invoking the corresponding methods (like doGet or doPost) depending on the HTTP method used (GET or POST).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following method sets application/context init parameter?

Options.OptionText: Its not possible in the method.

m_Item1.Explanation: Context parameters can not be set using any method. It can be done using web.xml deployment descriptor.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The life cycle of a servlet is managed by

Options.OptionText: The supporting protocol (such as http or https)

m_Item1.Explanation: Life Cycle of a Servlet The entire life cycle of a Servlet is managed by the Servlet container which uses the javax.servlet.Servlet interface to understand the Servlet object and manage it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The init parameter name and value pairs that are specified for a particular servlet are handled by
m_Item1.Explanation: ServletConfig object is used to retrieve information about config parameters. getServletConfig() method returns ServletConfg object created for the respective servlet

m_Item1.QuestionText: To get the context parameters configured in web.xml
m_Item1.Explanation: ServletContext object is used to read context parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not true for servlet ?
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlets are inherently multi-threaded. This means that a single servlet instance can handle multiple requests concurrently, each in its own thread.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When init() method of servlet gets called?

Options.OptionText: It is called everytime the servlet is requested
It is called whenever the servlet is requested for the first time
It needs to be called from doGet()/doPost() method

m_Item1.Explanation: The init () method is called once, when the servlet is first put into service. The destroy () method is called once, when the servlet is taken out of service. Neither is called on every request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the limit of data to be passed from HTML when doGet() method is used?
m_Item1.Explanation: To ensure broad compatibility across different browsers and even older systems, it's generally recommended to keep the total URL length (including the query string) under 2,000 to 2,048 characters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How is the dynamic interception of requests and responses is done to transform the information?
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet has various components like container, config, context, filter. Servlet filter provides the dynamic interception of requests and responses to transform the information.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The init( ) method takes ___________ object as a parameter.
m_Item1.Explanation:
An object of ServletConfig is created by the web container for each servlet. This object can be used to get configuration information from web.xml file.
If the configuration information is modified from the web.xml file, we don't need to change the servlet. So it is easier to manage the web application if any specific content is modified from time to time.
init() method is inherited from GenericServlet which has a ServletConfig as its parameter. thats how HttpServlet and what ever custom servlet you write by extending HttpServlet gets ServletConfig.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Java servlet, method init( ) is called ______ times.
m_Item1.Explanation: init() method gets called only once when first time request comes for servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a part of servlet life cycle?

Options.OptionText: Checking if an instance of servlet exists or not
Pausing the servlet of given period of time

m_Item1.Explanation: A servlet can be checked for existence, invoked and destroyed but can not be paused

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a J2EE client?
m_Item1.Explanation: J2EE clients are programs specifically designed to interact with J2EE servers and utilize J2EE features.  C++ program does not communicate with J2EE server in any way.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements are true?

Options.OptionText: A J2EE component runs on the J2EE container using the APIs provided by the Web container.
J2EE/Java EE applications aren't self contained. In order to be executed, they need to be deployed in a container. In other words, the container provides an execution environment on top of the JVM.
J2EE container provides a large number of services including invocation, configuration, security

m_Item1.Explanation: Java EE applications need to be deployed in web server in order to execute them after receiving the request from client side.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which part of a Java web project would you typically configure a servlet mapping?
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlet mappings are typically configured in the `web.xml` deployment descriptor file, specifying which servlet handles specific URLs.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which lifecycle method of a Java servlet gets called only once during its lifecycle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The init() method is called once when the servlet is instantiated. This method is used to initialize the servlet and is called before the servlet starts handling requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary function of a servlet in a Java web application?
m_Item1.Explanation: Servlets are Java programs that run on a server and are primarily used to generate dynamic web content in response to client requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which lifecycle method of a Java servlet is guaranteed to be called only once during a servlet's life?
m_Item1.Explanation: The `init()` method is called only once when a servlet is first loaded into memory, initializing it before handling any requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method in a servlet is called once for the entire lifecycle of the servlet, specifically when it is first loaded into memory?
m_Item1.Explanation: The init() method is called once when the servlet is first loaded into memory and is used to perform any one-time initialization tasks.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main purpose of the service() method in a servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The service() method is called to handle requests from clients and formulates an appropriate response based on the request type and parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the servlet lifecycle, which method is called just before the servlet is taken out of service?
m_Item1.Explanation: The destroy() method is called before the servlet is taken out of service and is used to perform any cleanup operations before the servlet is destroyed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which interface must a class implement to qualify as a servlet in Java?
m_Item1.Explanation: For a class to be a servlet in Java, it must implement the Servlet interface directly or indirectly through subclasses such as GenericServlet or HttpServlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method do you override in a servlet to handle a HTTP GET request?
m_Item1.Explanation: The doGet() method should be overridden to handle any HTTP GET requests and formulate an appropriate response.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods in the servlet lifecycle is called only once and is used for initialization?
m_Item1.Explanation: The init() method is called only once when the servlet is first loaded into memory and is used for initialization purposes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary function of the service() method in the servlet lifecycle?

Options.OptionText: To handle requests and generate responses

m_Item1.Explanation: The service() method is responsible for processing requests from clients and generating appropriate responses.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which lifecycle method is guaranteed to be called before the servlet is removed from service?
m_Item1.Explanation: The destroy() method is called once before a servlet is removed from service, allowing for cleanup of resources.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following directly invokes the service() method during the servlet lifecycle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The servlet container invokes the service() method each time a request is made to the servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if a servlet throws a ServletException during initialization?

Options.OptionText: The servlet will be terminated immediately
The exception is ignored and processing continues
The servlet will be marked unavailable

m_Item1.Explanation: If a ServletException is thrown during the init() method, the servlet is marked as unavailable and cannot service requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of overriding the destroy() method in a servlet?

Options.OptionText: To perform resource cleanup before the servlet is shut down

m_Item1.Explanation: Overriding the destroy() method allows for the cleanup of resources (such as closing database connections) before the servlet is terminated.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many times is the init() method called during the complete lifecycle of a servlet?

Options.OptionText: As many times as the servlet is accessed
Twice, once before and once after the servlet execution

m_Item1.Explanation: The init() method is called exactly once after the servlet has been instantiated, to perform any servlet-specific initialization required.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct method to initialize a servlet in the Servlet lifecycle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The init() method is called by the servlet container to indicate to a servlet that the servlet is being placed into service.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which object is used to communicate client requests to a servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: ServletRequest is an interface that provides a way to get information from the client, such as parameter name and value or even get the attribute from the session.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you retrieve initialization parameters from a web.xml file in a servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The getInitParameter() method is used to retrieve initialization parameters from the web.xml file in a servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods is invoked only once in the lifecycle of a servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The destroy() method is called only once at the end of the lifecycle of a servlet to allow it to clean up resources before being destroyed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What type of object is returned by the getServletContext() method?
m_Item1.Explanation: The getServletContext() method returns a ServletContext object, which enables communication with the servlet container and allows access to web application parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the HttpServlet class, which method should be overridden to handle HTTP POST requests?
m_Item1.Explanation: To handle HTTP POST requests, you should override the doPost() method in the HttpServlet class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which parameters does the doGet() method accept?
m_Item1.Explanation: doGet() method accepts HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse objects as parameters. HttpServletRequest object is used read information from client's request and HttpServletResponse is used to stuff the response content, response headers and cookies to be sent back to client.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the @WebServlet annotation?

Options.OptionText: To map a servlet to a URL pattern

m_Item1.Explanation: <font color="#0000ff">@WebServlet annotation is used to specify which request URL can be processed by the servlet. It is replacement of <servlet> and <servlet-mapping> elements of web.xml</font>

m_Item1.QuestionText: Write the question here123123
m_Item1.Explanation: Write the explanation here
